instruction: Read the experiment evidence and write one strict JSON experiment card with exactly these keys: run_id, dataset_name, model_name, verdict, primary_failure_modes, key_evidence, next_action. Output JSON only.
input:
Run metadata:
run_id: 20260504T080000Z
dataset_name: artifact-card-v1
model_name: unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit
train_loss: 1.688

Run summary:
A shorter-input artifact-card run kept JSON validity high and improved verdict selection, but failure-mode labels were still inconsistent on mixed cases.

Eval evidence:
- valid JSON stayed high across the eval split
- verdict labels improved on clear regressions and clear improvements
- mixed cases still flipped between same and improved

Operator note:
Better formatting, but mixed-case labeling is still weak.

Use only the evidence in this input.
output: {"run_id": "20260504T080000Z", "dataset_name": "artifact-card-v1", "model_name": "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit", "verdict": "improved", "primary_failure_modes": ["no-material-change", "missing-required-detail"], "key_evidence": ["the tuned model produced valid JSON more often than the base model", "label outputs were short but field accuracy stayed mixed"], "next_action": "add automatic field scoring before the next run"}

instruction: Read the experiment evidence and write one strict JSON experiment card with exactly these keys: run_id, dataset_name, model_name, verdict, primary_failure_modes, key_evidence, next_action. Output JSON only.
input:
Run metadata:
run_id: 20260504T081500Z
dataset_name: artifact-card-v1
model_name: unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit
train_loss: 1.742

Run summary:
Adding verbose operator notes caused the tuned model to drop canonical evidence phrases and miss one required field again.

Eval evidence:
- next_action was missing on several rows
- key_evidence became long and free-form
- JSON validity fell compared with the shorter-input baseline

Operator note:
The schema still breaks when the input becomes noisy.

Use only the evidence in this input.
output: {"run_id": "20260504T081500Z", "dataset_name": "artifact-card-v1", "model_name": "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit", "verdict": "regressed", "primary_failure_modes": ["missing-required-detail", "generic-explanation"], "key_evidence": ["longer inputs weakened schema discipline", "tuned answers became more generic than the reference"], "next_action": "shorten the inputs and keep the schema fixed before rerunning"}

instruction: Read the experiment evidence and write one strict JSON experiment card with exactly these keys: run_id, dataset_name, model_name, verdict, primary_failure_modes, key_evidence, next_action. Output JSON only.
input:
Run metadata:
run_id: 20260504T083000Z
dataset_name: artifact-card-v1
model_name: unsloth/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-bnb-4bit
train_loss: 1.655

Run summary:
A stronger base improved JSON validity and verdict labels, but the model still over-predicted extra failure modes on borderline examples.

Eval evidence:
- JSON validity stayed high
- verdict labels improved again
- primary_failure_modes often included one unsupported extra label

Operator note:
Bigger base, same label-discipline problem.

Use only the evidence in this input.
output: {"run_id": "20260504T083000Z", "dataset_name": "artifact-card-v1", "model_name": "unsloth/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-bnb-4bit", "verdict": "improved", "primary_failure_modes": ["fluency-without-correctness", "missing-required-detail"], "key_evidence": ["a stronger base improved fluency more than correctness", "the tuned model produced valid JSON more often than the base model"], "next_action": "add automatic field scoring before the next run"}

instruction: Read the experiment evidence and write one strict JSON experiment card with exactly these keys: run_id, dataset_name, model_name, verdict, primary_failure_modes, key_evidence, next_action. Output JSON only.
input:
Run metadata:
run_id: 20260504T084700Z
dataset_name: artifact-card-v1
model_name: unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit
train_loss: 1.751

Run summary:
A rerun with almost identical settings produced almost identical field failures again, especially on next_action and the second failure-mode label.

Eval evidence:
- loss barely moved
- the same two fields failed again
- qualitative outputs were almost unchanged

Operator note:
The dataset has probably become the bottleneck.

Use only the evidence in this input.
output: {"run_id": "20260504T084700Z", "dataset_name": "artifact-card-v1", "model_name": "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit", "verdict": "same", "primary_failure_modes": ["no-material-change", "missing-required-detail"], "key_evidence": ["repeated runs produced nearly identical outputs", "label outputs were short but field accuracy stayed mixed"], "next_action": "stop rerunning unchanged data and patch the weak concepts"}

instruction: Read the experiment evidence and write one strict JSON experiment card with exactly these keys: run_id, dataset_name, model_name, verdict, primary_failure_modes, key_evidence, next_action. Output JSON only.
input:
Run metadata:
run_id: 20260504T090200Z
dataset_name: issue-brief-v1
model_name: unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit
train_loss: 1.598

Run summary:
A note-to-brief run matched the template more often than the base model, but it still invented blockers and next steps on sparse notes.

Eval evidence:
- template matching improved
- invented blockers appeared on sparse notes
- base outputs were less structured but sometimes more faithful

Operator note:
Faithfulness remains the limiting issue.

Use only the evidence in this input.
output: {"run_id": "20260504T090200Z", "dataset_name": "issue-brief-v1", "model_name": "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit", "verdict": "inconclusive", "primary_failure_modes": ["hallucinated-detail", "missing-required-detail"], "key_evidence": ["the tuned model matched the target template more often than the base model", "tuned outputs invented details not present in the input"], "next_action": "shorten the inputs and keep the schema fixed before rerunning"}

instruction: Read the experiment evidence and write one strict JSON experiment card with exactly these keys: run_id, dataset_name, model_name, verdict, primary_failure_modes, key_evidence, next_action. Output JSON only.
input:
Run metadata:
run_id: 20260504T091900Z
dataset_name: failure-labeler-v1
model_name: unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit
train_loss: 1.389

Run summary:
A concise failure-labeling task stayed short and scoreable, but the tuned model still swapped overlap contamination with phrase-copy on some rows.

Eval evidence:
- outputs stayed short
- the label confusion persisted on closely related categories
- overall field accuracy improved only slightly

Operator note:
The label taxonomy may still need sharper boundaries.

Use only the evidence in this input.
output: {"run_id": "20260504T091900Z", "dataset_name": "failure-labeler-v1", "model_name": "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit", "verdict": "inconclusive", "primary_failure_modes": ["wrong-causal-point", "no-material-change"], "key_evidence": ["label outputs were short but field accuracy stayed mixed", "loss improved but held-out answers still missed the target concept"], "next_action": "add automatic field scoring before the next run"}

instruction: Read the experiment evidence and write one strict JSON experiment card with exactly these keys: run_id, dataset_name, model_name, verdict, primary_failure_modes, key_evidence, next_action. Output JSON only.
input:
Run metadata:
run_id: 20260504T093100Z
dataset_name: artifact-card-v1
model_name: unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit
train_loss: 1.768

Run summary:
Adding near-duplicate cases for one mixed verdict pattern caused the model to copy evidence wording into the failure-mode field again.

Eval evidence:
- new training rows were very close to the held-out phrasing
- failure-mode labels repeated evidence text
- field accuracy did not improve

Operator note:
This looks like overlap contamination inside a structured task.

Use only the evidence in this input.
output: {"run_id": "20260504T093100Z", "dataset_name": "artifact-card-v1", "model_name": "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct-bnb-4bit", "verdict": "regressed", "primary_failure_modes": ["overlap-contaminated-eval", "phrase-copy-or-template-collapse"], "key_evidence": ["train/eval overlap made the improvement untrustworthy", "phrase-copy distortion appeared in the tuned output"], "next_action": "remove overlap-heavy rows and rerun"}

instruction: Read the experiment evidence and write one strict JSON experiment card with exactly these keys: run_id, dataset_name, model_name, verdict, primary_failure_modes, key_evidence, next_action. Output JSON only.
input:
Run metadata:
run_id: 20260504T094500Z
dataset_name: artifact-card-v1
model_name: unsloth/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-bnb-4bit
train_loss: 1.649

Run summary:
A 3B rerun improved fluency and verdict consistency, but key_evidence still drifted into free-form prose on ambiguous rows.

Eval evidence:
- verdict consistency improved
- key_evidence became longer on ambiguous rows
- JSON validity remained strong

Operator note:
The remaining problem is target normalization, not raw fluency.

Use only the evidence in this input.
output: {"run_id": "20260504T094500Z", "dataset_name": "artifact-card-v1", "model_name": "unsloth/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-bnb-4bit", "verdict": "improved", "primary_failure_modes": ["fluency-without-correctness", "missing-required-detail"], "key_evidence": ["a stronger base improved fluency more than correctness", "the tuned model produced valid JSON more often than the base model"], "next_action": "add automatic field scoring before the next run"}
